sentences.english: Hello and welcome to The English We Speak, where we explain phrases used by fluent English speakers so that you can use them, too!
sentences.korean: 안녕하세요, 유창한 영어 화자들이 쓰는 표현을 설명하는 The English We Speak에 오신 것을 환영합니다!

sentences.english: I'm Feifei, and I'm joined by Georgie.
Hi Feifei! How's your day going?
Not too bad, thank you.
What about you?
Well, actually… not great.
I was taking out the bins this morning and there were maggots everywhere!
sentences.korean: 오늘 아침에 쓰레기를 버리러 나갔는데 구더기가 여기저기 있었어!

sentences.english: It was disgusting – it made my skin crawl.
Oh, Georgie! You just said the maggots 'made your skin crawl', and that's the expression we're learning in this programme, but what does it mean?
sentences.korean: 오, 조지! 방금 구더기가 '소름 끼쳤다'고 했지. 그게 이번 프로그램에서 배울 표현인데, 무슨 뜻이야?

sentences.english: OK, well I'm sure our listeners know what skin is, but Feifei, what does crawl mean?
sentences.korean: 좋아, 청취자들은 skin이 뭔지 알겠지만, 페이페이, crawl은 무슨 뜻이지?

quiz.question: What does '징그러운 벌레들' mean in English?

sentences.english: Well, 'crawl' is a verb and it's a way of moving your body along the ground, for example, babies crawl on their hands and knees.
sentences.korean: 'crawl'은 동사로 바닥을 따라 몸을 이동하는 방법이야.

sentences.english: And animals can crawl too – and it can be quite a creepy movement when they do so!
sentences.korean: 예를 들어 아기들은 손과 무릎으로 기어다니지. 동물도 기어다닐 수 있는데, 그게 꽤 소름 끼치는 움직임이야!

sentences.english: That's right! So, we even have a nickname for bugs which is 'creepy crawlies'.
And, if something makes our skin crawl, it means you have the sensation of horrible insects crawling over your body – that doesn't sound very nice, does it?!
sentences.korean: 무언가가 소름 끼치게 한다면, 끔찍한 곤충이 몸 위를 기어다니는 것 같은 느낌이라는 뜻이야 — 별로 안 좋겠지?!

sentences.english: No, it doesn't… So, if something makes your skin crawl, it means you're incredibly disgusted or frightened by it.
sentences.korean: 별로지… 그러니까 무언가가 소름 끼치게 한다면, 엄청나게 역겹거나 무섭다는 뜻이야.

quiz.question: What does '징그러운 벌레들' mean in English?

sentences.english: Let's hear some examples.
I watched a film about an alien that attacked a group of people on a spaceship.
sentences.korean: 우주선에서 외계인이 사람들을 공격하는 영화를 봤는데, 외계인이 정말 소름 끼쳤어.

sentences.english: The alien made my skin crawl.
sentences.korean: 디 에일리언 메이드 마이 스킨 크롤.

sentences.english: My brother changes his bed sheets about three times a year.
sentences.korean: 형은 침대 시트를 1년에 세 번 정도 갈아.

sentences.english: It makes my skin crawl just thinking about it.

quiz.question: What does '징그러운 벌레들' mean in English?

sentences.english: I saw a news story about a plague of mice.
sentences.korean: 생쥐 떼에 대한 뉴스를 봤는데, 수천 마리가 사람들의 집을 돌아다니고 있었어.

sentences.english: There were thousands of them running through people's houses and it just made my skin crawl to see it.
Georgie, what kind of reaction did you have when you found those maggots this morning?
sentences.korean: 조지, 오늘 아침에 그 구더기를 발견했을 때 어떤 반응이었어?

sentences.english: … I don't want to think about it.
My whole body shuddered and shook.
Like your skin was physically crawling!
So, to remember this expression, you can either think about insects crawling over your skin, or that your skin itself crawls in disgust.
sentences.korean: 이 표현을 기억하려면, 곤충이 피부 위를 기어다니는 걸 상상하거나, 피부 자체가 역겨움에 꿈틀거리는 걸 생각하면 돼.

sentences.english: Let's have a quick recap.
If something 'makes your skin crawl', it means it disgusts you or frightens you – it gives you a horrible physical sensation.
sentences.korean: 'makes your skin crawl'은 역겹거나 무서워서 끔찍한 신체 반응을 일으킨다는 뜻이야.

sentences.english: Thank you for listening!
Bye!
Bye!

quiz.question: What does '역겨운, 구역질 나는' mean in English?
